programme.title: Live: College Track and Field
programme.desc: From Hayward Field in Eugene, Ore.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter. The "NFL Live" crew entertains fans while offering all the latest news and analysis from across the NFL.

programme.title: Women's Sports Now
programme.desc: Covering topics in women's sports; expert insights and insider coverage of the week's most newsworthy moments-from NCAA basketball, NWSL, the WNBA Draft, Wimbledon, and more; guests bring their unique POV to the week's discussion.

programme.title: X Games Salt Lake City 2025
programme.desc: Action from the 2025 X Games, held in Salt Lake City from June 27-29.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: MLB Baseball
programme.desc: Seattle and Baltimore play the finale of their 4-game series at Oriole Park at Camden Yards. Bryan Woo and Kyle Bradish are the expected starters in this clash. The last five games for both teams are balanced with two wins and three losses.

programme.title: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." The show also delivers what "SportsCente

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: ESPN FC
programme.desc: Highlights, news, reactions and opinions from the day in soccer.

programme.title: Live: Get Up
programme.desc: ESPN's morning show features host Mike Greenberg with a rotating and diverse group of analysts opining about and analyzing the latest news from the world of sports. The two-hour, personality-driven show originates from Hudson Square in Lower Manhattan, a modern media hub for ESPN and The Walt Disney Company's New York-based teams. Greenberg, aka "Greeny," is formerly one-half of ESPN Radio's longt

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: NCAA Men's College World Series Studio
programme.desc: College baseball experts help viewers stay informed about NCAA Men's College World Series action.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: NBA Today
programme.desc: In-depth, comprehensive daily coverage of all aspects of the NBA from expert analysts.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter. The "NFL Live" crew entertains fans while offering all the latest news and analysis from across the NFL.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter. The "NFL Live" crew entertains fans while offering all the latest news and analysis from across the NFL.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: Live: NCAA Men's College World Series Studio
programme.desc: College baseball experts help viewers stay informed about NCAA Men's College World Series action.

programme.title: Fútbol Americas
programme.desc: A bi-weekly soccer debate show hosted by Sebastian Salazar and Herculez Gomez with a focus on the young promising generations of the USMNT and EL TRI from both the club and country perspective.

programme.title: Live: College Track and Field
programme.desc: From Hayward Field in Eugene, Ore.

programme.title: E60
programme.desc: A year in the life of transgender high school track and field sprinter Matt Dawkins, an athlete just trying to be himself.

programme.title: Live: ESPN FC
programme.desc: Highlights, news, reactions and opinions from the day in soccer.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter. The "NFL Live" crew entertains fans while offering all the latest news and analysis from across the NFL.

programme.title: Believers: Boston Red Sox
programme.desc: Red Sox fans share decades of losses and heartbreak until 2004, when their team faces the Yankees in the ALCS; one moment changes everything.

programme.title: Believers: Boston Red Sox
programme.desc: Boston's complex history of progress and prejudice finds reflection in its baseball team; the 2004 Red Sox, facing elimination, begin dismantling Yankees' dominance, uniting fans and challenging an alleged curse.

programme.title: Believers: Boston Red Sox
programme.desc: Red Sox fans trade generations of curse-driven misery for triumph; their identity shifts from suffering to success, leaving them to navigate unfamiliar territory where winning is expected.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: Filmmaker Barbara Kopple examines the legacy of baseball owner George M. Steinbrenner, including the building of a new Yankee Stadium and the transition of ownership to his heirs, led by his younger son, Hal.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The San Francisco area is rocked by an earthquake shortly before the start of Game 3 of the 1989 World Series.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Learning the fundamentals of flats fishing and the advanced tactics used by proven tournament anglers.

programme.title: George Poveromo's World of Saltwater Fishing
programme.desc: George Poveromo has a go at the redfish, sea trout and other game fish found within the Charleston Harbor complex.

programme.title: Goliath Fishing
programme.desc: Captain James Marko chases fish all around south Florida.

programme.title: Motorhead Garage
programme.desc: Dave Dobson hosts TV's only automotive aftermarket showcase, featuring the latest products to make vehicles look cooler, perform better, or last longer.

programme.title: Last Train to North America
programme.desc: The journey begins aboard a train to Toronto as the series sets the stage for a continent preparing to host the world.

programme.title: Last Train to North America
programme.desc: The series travels to the United States with stops in Boston and New York, exploring two historic sports cities preparing for the global spotlight.

programme.title: Live: ESPN FC
programme.desc: Highlights, news, reactions and opinions from the day in soccer.

programme.title: Banana Ball World Tour
programme.desc: From the ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex in Bay Lake, Florida.

programme.title: Live: Athletes Unlimited Softball League
programme.desc: From The Stadium at the Parkway Bank Sports Complex in Rosemont, Ill.

programme.title: Cornhole
programme.desc: From Will Rogers Memorial Center in Fort Worth, Texas.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: Rafael Palmeiro and Will Clark, teammates for the 1985 Mississippi State Bulldogs, nearly propelled the team to a College World Series title.

programme.title: Live: NCAA Men's College World Series Studio
programme.desc: College baseball experts help viewers stay informed about NCAA Men's College World Series action.

programme.title: Live: College Track and Field
programme.desc: From Hayward Field in Eugene, Ore.

programme.title: 2026 Stanley Cup Final

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: The Pat McAfee Show
programme.desc: Former NFL punter Pat McAfee brings his humor and candor to the microphone to highlight the day's top stories.

programme.title: Live: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: The Troy Trojans and the West Virginia Mountaineers face-off in Omaha, Nebraska in the 2026 Men's College World Series. Troy's Jimmy Janicki leads his team with 19 home runs, while Gavin Kelly leads West Virginia with 17 home runs.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. programme.title: Live: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: The Ole Miss Rebels battle the North Carolina Tar Heels in a Men's College World Series game. Hunter Elliott, the strikeout leader for Ole Miss, faces North Carolina home run leader Cooper Nicholson in Omaha, Nebraska.

programme.title: Live: Athletes Unlimited Softball League
programme.desc: From Smith Family Stadium in Durham, N.C.

programme.title: Live: NCAA Men's College World Series Studio
programme.desc: College baseball experts help viewers stay informed about NCAA Men's College World Series action.

programme.title: Live: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: The Oklahoma Sooners take on Alabama Crimson Tide in the Men's College World Series. Alabama punched its first ticket to the tournament since 1999 by beating St. John's, while Oklahoma swept Kansas in the Lawrence Super Regional to lock its spot.

programme.title: E60
programme.desc: The award-winning "E:60" features the best stories in sports. Hosted by Jeremy Schaap and Lisa Salters, this 60-minute ESPN original is a mix of all-access profiles with the biggest names, the most compelling stories from off the field of play, in-depth investigative reports and lighthearted tales, all presented with a cinematic sensibility.

programme.title: NBA Tip-Off
programme.desc: Host Ernie Johnson and analysts Shaquille O'Neal, Charles Barkley and Kenny Smith ready viewers for NBA action before ESPN's Wednesday night doubleheaders (and other select telecasts). Guest analysts also are featured from time to time.

programme.title: Live: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: The Texas Longhorns and Georgia Bulldogs meet in Game 4 of the Men's College World Series. The Bulldogs rely on Daniel Jackson and his .396 batting average, while the Longhorns look to Anthony Pack Jr. who leads the team with his .359 average.

programme.title: Live: CFL Football
programme.desc: The Winnipeg Blue Bombers and Hamilton Tiger-Cats square off in a regular-season matchup. Host Blue Bombers lean on running back Brady Oliveira after his 113-yard performance, while the Tiger-Cats rely on Bo Levi Mitchell and his 307 passing yards.

programme.title: UFC Countdown
programme.desc: A look at the lives and training camps of UFC athletes as they prepare for the next UFC pay-per-view event.

programme.title: UFC Reloaded
programme.desc: The replay of UFC pay-per-views and Fight Night events.

programme.title: UFC Main Event
programme.desc: We take a look back at Alexander Volkanovski and Max Holloway's UFC 276 bout as the pair met in the featherweight division for the third time back in July 2022.

programme.title: UFC Main Event
programme.desc: At UFC 309, former lightweight champion Charles Oliveira faces Michael Chandler in a rematch at Madison Square Garden.

programme.title: Live: Boomer and Gio
programme.desc: CBS Sports Network simulcasts WFAN's "Boomer and Gio." Former NFL MVP Boomer Esiason and longtime radio personality Gregg Giannotti tackle the latest sports news and headlines.

programme.title: UFC Countdown
programme.desc: A look at the lives and training camps of UFC athletes as they prepare for the next UFC pay-per-view event.

programme.title: UFC Fight Flashback
programme.desc: UFC Fight Flashback is an enhanced replay of some of the most memorable title fights in UFC history, featuring never-before-seen footage from state-of-the-art cameras, and exclusive new sound.

programme.title: Early Edge
programme.desc: The Early Edge breaks down the day's biggest games, giving sports fans the best bets -- from long shots to sure things.

programme.title: The Joe & Ruffino Show
programme.desc: Blake Ruffino and Joe DeLeone join forces to talk about the latest in the SEC and College Football.

programme.title: CBS Sports HQ Spotlight
programme.desc: Sports news and highlights.

programme.title: CBS Sports HQ Spotlight
programme.desc: Sports news and highlights.

programme.title: Live: AVP Beach Volleyball
programme.desc: Coverage of beach volleyball competitions from around the United States.

programme.title: UFC Countdown
programme.desc: A look at the lives and training camps of UFC athletes as they prepare for the next UFC pay-per-view event.

programme.title: UFC Reloaded
programme.desc: Bantamweight champion Aljamain Sterling defends his title against fighting rival, Sean O'Malley. Also, Zhang Weili goes head-to-head with veteran Amanda Lemos.

programme.title: UFC - The Walk
programme.desc: Go behind-the-scenes with the UFC's biggest stars in the lead up to some of the biggest fights of their careers.

programme.title: UFC Ultimate Knockouts
programme.desc: A look at Dana White's contender series alumni.

programme.title: UFC Countdown
programme.desc: A look at the lives and training camps of UFC athletes as they prepare for the next UFC pay-per-view event.

programme.title: UFC Reloaded
programme.desc: Newly crowned bantamweight champion Sean O'Malley has his first title defense against the only man to beat him, top contender Marlon Vera. Plus, Dustin Poirier takes on Benoit Saint Denis and Michael Page makes his debut against Kevin Holland.

programme.title: PBA Bowling
programme.desc: Action from the PBA bowling tour.

programme.title: PBA Bowling
programme.desc: From Bowlero Brooklyn Park in Brooklyn Park, Minn.

programme.title: PBA Bowling
programme.desc: From Bowlero Brooklyn Park in Brooklyn Park, Minn.

programme.title: PBA Bowling
programme.desc: Action from the PBA bowling tour.

programme.title: Disc Golf Pro Tour
programme.desc: The top professionals compete for prize money on the Disc Golf Pro Tour.

programme.title: Watercross
programme.desc: Pro watercross racers take on the course at Lake Crook State Park in Paris, Texas.

programme.title: Live: Athletes Unlimited Softball League
programme.desc: From Dumke Family Softball Stadium in Salt Lake City.

programme.title: World of Outlaws
programme.desc: Eastaboga, Ala.

programme.title: World of Outlaws
programme.desc: From Pevely, Mo.

programme.title: Live: CFL Football
programme.desc: The BC Lions face the Saskatchewan Roughriders in Week 2 of the CFL regular season. Quarterback Nathan Rourke commands the Lions against Trevor Harris and the Roughriders as the West Division contenders clash on the gridiron.
